frameworks.name: dopamine D2 receptor neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: D2R signaling in PFC gates learning, flexibility, and motivation

frameworks.name: prefrontal neural population coding
frameworks.one_line: direction-selective spiking encodes visuomotor associations under D2R control

frameworks.name: cortical alpha oscillation dynamics
frameworks.one_line: alpha-band LFP indexes prefrontal disengagement under D2R blockade

frameworks.name: schizophrenia cognitive dysfunction
frameworks.one_line: motivates D2R-PFC link to affective and cognitive symptoms

core_question: How do prefrontal D2 receptors modulate executive functions and neural coding during visuomotor associative learning in monkeys?
key_assumptions: lateral PFC D2R causally mediates executive functions
local eticlopride injection selectively blocks D2R in PFC
spiking selectivity and LFP power index circuit computation
manipulations: local D2R antagonist (eticlopride) injection into lateral PFC
novel versus highly familiar visuomotor associations
left versus right saccade target mapping
key_findings: D2R blockade impaired associative learning and cognitive flexibility
D2R blockade reduced motivation but spared familiar-association performance
eticlopride reduced saccade-direction selectivity of prefrontal neurons
D2R blockade decreased neural information and increased alpha oscillations
interpretations: prefrontal D2R supports new learning and flexibility but not consolidated behavior
D2R loss in PFC degrades task-relevant coding and elevates idling alpha rhythms
